frame_analyses.description: Initial setup with green foam block visible, curved suture needle present, instruments positioned around foam block
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean workspace setup, needle appears to be curved suturing needle

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle, suture tail visible extending from foam
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp position approximately 2/3 from tip

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned for entry through foam block, suture thread visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled needle positioning, appropriate entry angle

frame_analyses.description: Needle has penetrated through foam block with suture following, both ends of suture now visible
Beginning knot tying sequence with suture ends being manipulated by instruments
Continuing first throw formation with instruments manipulating suture ends
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper instrument technique for intracorporeal knot tying

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being formed, can see knot taking shape on foam surface
Final throw being completed, knot appears secure on foam block
Scissors being used to cut suture ends, knot appears complete and secure
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate cutting technique leaving adequate tail length

knot_assessments.notes: Final throw completed with hand switch

confidence_rationale: Good visibility of overall task completion and knot formation. Unable to clearly identify specific suture marks on foam block due to camera angle and foam texture, but no obvious deviations observed.
cannot_determine: Exact location of suture marks on foam block - foam texture and camera angle make precise measurement difficult
Whether this is actually a Penrose drain with slit or foam block substitute - appears to be foam training block rather than drain
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level performance with very fast completion time of 44 seconds. Clean technique throughout all phases with proper hand switching between throws.
strengths: Extremely efficient needle loading and placement
Smooth knot tying sequence with proper hand alternation
No apparent technical errors or fumbles
improvement_suggestions: Performance appears expert-level with minimal room for improvement
Maintain this level of efficiency and technique
